findings.title: Settlement executes caller-supplied interaction bytes that are not bound to the signed order payload

best_agent_match.title: All token conversions use zero slippage protection and are trivially sandwichable

findings.title: Self-targeted settlement interactions allow reentrancy that satisfies `allowedSender = SETTLEMENT`

best_agent_match.title: Caller-controlled payer/source address can make settlement spend its own inventory or third-party approvals

findings.title: Unchecked dynamic offset and length parsing enables calldata corruption and replay of historical orders

best_agent_match.title: All token conversions use zero slippage protection and are trivially sandwichable

findings.title: Settlement releases real taker assets when a malicious maker token lies about transfers and balances

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can trigger the exploit routine against the contract's prefunded 1M native balance

rejected_candidates.title: Caller-controlled payer/source address can make settlement spend its own inventory or third-party approvals
rejected_candidates.reason: The trailing encoded address is demonstrably attacker-controlled, but the available source does not establish that it is the payer/source field rather than another settlement parameter. The realistic harm from attacker-controlled call targets and settlement self-calls is already captured by F-001/F-002, so this candidate is too speculative as a distinct issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can trigger the exploit routine against the contract's prefunded 1M native balance
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an access-control issue in the PoC verifier contract, not a protocol vulnerability in the settlement system under audit.

rejected_candidates.title: All token conversions use zero slippage protection and are trivially sandwichable
rejected_candidates.reason: This concerns the verifier's post-exploit swap helpers, not the protocol under audit. It does not show a reportable flaw in the settlement contracts.

rejected_candidates.title: No withdrawal or recovery path permanently locks the prefunded ETH and all proceeds
rejected_candidates.reason: This is also specific to the PoC verifier harness rather than the settlement protocol, so it is out of scope for the protocol-level findings list.
